mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is pinned near zero while CPIAUCSL continues to print positive monthly inflation, markets price eventual policy normalization and higher term premium. Over the next 20 trading days this combination pushes the 10-year Treasury yield upward as rates repricing dominates any growth support.
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS remains near zero but the 10-year yield falls in a majority of 20-day windows during positive CPIAUCSL months, or if the sign of the response is opposite in more than 60% of similar episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above a deteriorating labor-market threshold signals weaker future demand and stronger safe-haven flows into Treasuries. Once UNRATE rises enough to validate recession risk, the 10-year yield should fall over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day Treasury yield changes are not negative in triggered labor-deterioration windows, or if yields rise in more than 60% of instances after the trigger fires.

mechanism_narrative: A broad improvement in PAYEMS together with rising RSAFS indicates stronger household income and spending, which lifts expected real activity and inflation compensation. This interaction should push the 10-year Treasury yield higher over the next 20 trading days because both demand and income momentum are jointly strengthening.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-RSAFS signal does not outperform either series alone and the 10-year yield rises in fewer than 55% of top-quartile interaction episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO after prior weakness signals improving capacity utilization and a less slack industrial sector. In a recovery phase, this reduces deflation fear and increases the expected path of rates, so the 10-year Treasury yield rises over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO momentum fails to precede higher 10-year yields in at least 55% of 5-day windows or if the average yield change is non-positive across recovery episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST is extremely depressed, markets infer weak housing demand and slower aggregate activity, which raises the probability of easier policy and lower long rates. The 10-year yield should fall over the next 20 trading days after the housing-starts trough is confirmed.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 12-month moving average by more than 15%
failure_condition: Reject if threshold-crossing housing weakness does not produce negative 20-day yield responses, or if the yield rises in a majority of post-threshold observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in CPIAUCSL while FEDFUNDS remains anchored creates a policy-lag gap that forces the market to reprice future tightening. This should lift the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 20 trading days because inflation surprises dominate the current stance of policy.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates above its prior 6-month average while FEDFUNDS stays unchanged
failure_condition: Reject if CPIAUCSL surprises without subsequent yield increases in most triggered cases, or if the joint inflation-policy gap has no incremental explanatory power over CPIAUCSL alone.

mechanism_narrative: A regime of falling PAYEMS with rising UNRATE marks an unambiguous payroll contraction and labor slackening. That combination should lower expected growth and inflation, pulling the 10-year Treasury yield down over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined payroll-loss plus unemployment-rise signal does not produce negative 60-day yield direction in a majority of late-cycle observations.

mechanism_narrative: Strong simultaneous growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS indicates that both output and spending are accelerating, which should raise real-rate expectations and term premium. Over the next 20 trading days this macro surprise should push the 10-year Treasury yield upward.
failure_condition: Reject if joint GDP-retail acceleration does not coincide with higher 10-year yields in at least 55% of 60-day evaluation windows, or if the interaction term adds no predictive lift.
